marketState: neutral-bearish; extreme near-term heat premium offsetting structural storage surplus and seasonal headwinds
summary: Front-month Aug NG=F at $3.279 is sitting just $0.319 above the $2.960 model fair value, within a wide $2.460–$3.460 band, supported almost entirely by an extraordinary 1-7D weather demand spike that is above the 5-year maximum (+45.4% vs 5y, rangePos 1.71). The 8-16D outlook already shows a sharp demand pullback (14.3% vs 5y, rangePos 0.95), signaling the heat premium is waning. Longer-term, seasonality is a headwind: 4-week upside probability is only 19% with a typical -1.8% move from this calendar week, and storage is a hefty +151 Bcf above the 5-year average after a slightly bearish injection surprise. The one structural bull card is COT positioning — managed money sits at the 18th 5-year percentile (deeply net short at -82,722), creating short-squeeze tail risk that keeps a tight ceiling on the short thesis. The futures curve is in modest near-term backwardation (Sep-Oct below Aug) before steep winter contango (Jan 2027 +$1.15 vs front), which tells the same story: summer demand fades, winter supply tightness is the real bull narrative.

longTerm.entry: $3.26–$3.30 (current zone; enter as weather premium decays)
longTerm.stop: $3.50 (above $3.46 upper fair-value band; a close here on sustained heat would invalidate)
longTerm.tp1: $2.96 (model fair value; first gravity pull as heat fades)
longTerm.tp2: $2.65 (seasonal correction zone, consistent with -7% to -10% from peak)
longTerm.rationale: The 4-week upside probability is only 19% — the weakest point of the cooling-season window — with a typical -1.8% seasonal move and low reliability (51 pp spread); this alone argues against buying at elevated levels. Storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise (+6 Bcf vs forecast) anchors a supply-heavy backdrop that will weigh on prices once air-conditioning load subsides in late July through August. Price is above the $2.96 fair value and the fair value itself only rises to $3.10 by day 21, meaning the current premium shrinks naturally without needing a price collapse.
longTerm.invalidation: Sustained daily close above $3.50 driven by extended heat dome, a major supply disruption (hurricane, freeze), or a COT squeeze where managed-money net shorts reverse rapidly to above the 50th percentile.

shortTerm.rationale: The 1-7D weather demand is at an extreme — above the 5-year maximum range — yet price is barely holding its prior close ($3.279 = flat), suggesting the heat spike is already priced in. Shorting into above-max heat with managed money at the 18th COT percentile (crowded short) risks a violent squeeze. Simultaneously, the 8-16D demand outlook rolls off sharply to 14.3% vs 5y and the 24h trend is already -0.6 Bcf/d, so going long here chases a waning catalyst at a slight fair-value premium.
shortTerm.trigger: Enter SHORT on any bounce to $3.40+ (sell the heat squeeze): stop $3.55, TP1 $3.10, TP2 $2.96 (~1:2 R:R). Alternatively enter LONG on a pullback to $3.10–$3.15 IF 8-16D demand forecast re-strengthens above the 5y maximum: stop $2.97, TP1 $3.35 (~1:1.7 R:R). Wait for one of these setups to resolve; do not chase current mid-range price.
